steps.title: Identify what x and f(x) represent
steps.content: From the problem context, $f(x) = 8,000(1+0.04)^x$ gives the value of the CD in dollars, where $x$ is the number of years after the investment was made.

steps.title: Interpret f(2) using function notation
steps.content: When we write $f(2)$, we substitute $x = 2$ into the function. Since $x$ represents the number of years, $f(2)$ gives the value of the CD exactly 2 years after the initial investment. The problem states this value is approximately 8,652.80 dollars.

steps.title: Match to the correct interpretation
steps.content:
Choice A — The value of the CD would be approximately 8,652.80 dollars if invested at 2% interest. refers to 2% interest, but the function uses 4% (since $1 + 0.04 = 1.04$) — incorrect.
Choice B — 8,652.80 days after the investment was made, the value of the CD was approximately 2 times greater than its initial value. refers to days and doubling — doesn't match what we found — incorrect.
Choice D — 2 years after the investment was made, the value of the CD was approximately 8,652.80 dollars greater than its initial value. says 8,652.80 is how much more than the initial value (a gain), but 8,652.80 is the total value, not the gain amount — incorrect.
Choice C — 2 years after the investment was made, the value of the CD was approximately 8,652.80 dollars. states: 2 years after the investment was made, the value of the CD was approximately 8,652.80 dollars. This is exactly what $f(2)$ represents. That matches Choice C — 2 years after the investment was made, the value of the CD was approximately 8,652.80 dollars..
